item.title: Unilever in talks to combine food business with McCormick, WSJ reports
item.description: Unilever is in talks to separate its food business and combine it ​with spice maker McCormick in an all-stock deal ‌that could come within weeks, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday, citing sources.

item.title: FCC approves combination of Nexstar and Tegna TV stations
item.description: The Federal Communications Commission said on ​Thursday it has approved the sale ‌of some local broadcast TV stations from Tegna to Nexstar .

item.title: Nexstar's $3.5 billion Tegna deal cleared by US DOJ, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice has unconditionally cleared Nexstar's $3.5 billion deal to acquire rival ​Tegna , Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing ‌people familiar with the matter.

item.title: OpenAI plans desktop 'superapp' to simplify user experience, WSJ reports
item.description: OpenAI is planning ​to unify ‌its ChatGPT app, coding ​platform Codex ​and browser into ⁠a desktop "superapp" ​to simplify ​user experience, the Wall Street ​Journal reported ​on Thursday.

item.title: US charges three people with conspiring to divert AI tech to China
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department said on Thursday that three ‌people have been charged with conspiring to unlawfully divert U.S. artificial intelligence technology to China.

item.title: Locked out BP workers picket outside Indiana refinery amid labor contract dispute
item.description: Workers ‌at BP’s Whiting, Indiana, refinery began picketing on Thursday after the British oil major barred more than 800 union members from entering the facility, following months of stalled ​contract negotiations.

item.title: High-speed combat drone production starts at new US Anduril plant in days
item.description: Anduril Industries will begin building its new FURY, "loyal wingman," high-speed combat drones ‌in the coming days at a new facility in Ohio, as the U.S. military's interest in unmanned aircraft surges following battlefield successes in Ukraine and Iran.

item.title: US SEC forming new team to police accounting issues
item.description: Wall Street's top regulator is forming a new ‌enforcement unit for accounting matters while cutting staff at an outside watchdog formed to police the accounting industry in the wake of the Enron-era scandals of the early 2000s, according to job postings and a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Nvidia to sell 1 million chips to Amazon by end of 2027 in cloud deal
item.description: Nvidia will sell 1 million of its graphics processing unit chips, along with a host of the AI giant's other ​offerings, to Amazon.com's cloud computing unit by 2027, a Nvidia executive ‌told Reuters on Thursday.

item.title: Prices for oil, fuel cargoes smash record highs as Iran war chokes Middle East supply
item.description: If you want to buy a cargo of oil in Asia or jet fuel in ​Europe right now, you may have to pay a record price for it.

item.title: Trading Day: Bracing for global rate hikes
item.description: Wall Street fell on Thursday in highly volatile trading that saw huge swings in world stocks, bonds and oil prices, as traders began to price in global interest rate hikes to counter the inflationary pressures of the Middle East energy crisis.

item.title: NTSB investigating close call between FedEx, Alaska Airlines jets
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board said ​on Thursday it is ‌investigating a close call in which an Alaska ​Airlines flight overflew ​a FedEx airplane while both ⁠jets were attempting ​to land on crossing ​runways at Newark Liberty International Airport.

item.title: Apollo Global commits $1 billion for retail joint venture with Realty Income
item.description: Realty Income said on Thursday it ​will get $1 billion from asset manager ‌Apollo Global Management and affiliates for a 49% interest in a new joint venture that ​is expected to own a portfolio ​of single-tenant retail properties with long-term net ⁠leases.

item.title: US, Japan to focus rare earths cooperation on select group of minerals at first
item.description: The U.S. and Japan on Thursday released an action plan for their efforts to develop alternatives to China for critical minerals and ​rare earths supply chains, focusing initially on price floors for ‌a select group of minerals.

item.title: FedEx raises full-year forecast after solid holiday quarter
item.description: FedEx , known for its air-dependent overnight deliveries, on Thursday raised its full-year profit forecast as it reported a rise in third-quarter profit ​and revenue, helped by a surge in deliveries during the ‌all-important holiday quarter.

item.title: Cheniere, Venture Global shares surge amid Iran attacks on Qatar LNG infrastructure
item.description: Shares of U.S. liquefied natural gas producer Cheniere Energy jumped to an all-time high while ​Venture Global initially spiked as much as 13% on Thursday after QatarEnergy said attacks by Iran may shut nearly a fifth of its ‌LNG output for up to five years.

item.title: US pump prices jump 30% since Middle East war began, headed toward $4 a gallon
item.description: U.S. gasoline pump prices have ​jumped more than 30% this month, moving toward $4 a gallon despite efforts by President Donald Trump to curb ‌price increases and contain supply disruptions stemming from the Middle East war.

item.title: Jeff Bezos aims to raise $100 billion to buy, revamp manufacturing firms with AI, WSJ reports
item.description: Jeff Bezos is in early discussions to raise $100 billion for a new fund that would acquire ​manufacturing companies and seek to use AI to ‌drive and speed up automation, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday.

item.title: Exclusive: German semiconductor group Elmos exploring a sale, sources say
item.description: Germany's ​Elmos Semiconductor is exploring a sale as its founders consider exiting the business, according to ‌three people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Goldman Sachs to make small job cuts on performance in April, source says
item.description: Goldman Sachs plans ​to cut a ‌small number of jobs in April of ​staff that have ​underperformed, a source familiar ⁠with the ​matter said.

item.title: Talk of AI disruption, wars, oil and rates dominates M&A conference even as deals soar
item.description: The value of dealmaking is close to all-time highs, even as corporate executives struggle to navigate ‌the Trump administration's tariffs, wars on multiple continents and how artificial intelligence will shape the economy in the years ahead.

item.title: How US regulators are overhauling bank capital rules
item.description: U.S. banking regulators on Thursday unveiled sweeping plans to streamline and ease numerous capital requirements for the nation's largest banks, which could free up billions of dollars for lending, dividends and share buybacks.

item.title: Ecolab nearing deal to acquire KKR's data center cooling company for up to $5 billion, WSJ reports
item.description: Ecolab ​is nearing a ‌deal to acquire data center cooling company ​CoolIT Systems from ​KKR for between $4.5 billion ⁠and $5 billion, the ​Wall Street Journal reported ​on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US fixed 30-year mortgage rate hits three-month high amid Iran war
item.description: The average rate on the popular U.S. 30-year fixed-rate mortgage ​surged to a three-month high ‌this week as war in the Middle East stoked inflation fears, dealing a blow ​to the Trump administration's efforts ​to make housing more affordable.

item.title: Apple fends off bid for new Apple Watch import ban at US trade tribunal
item.description: A U.S. trade tribunal preliminarily ruled that Apple's current Apple Watches do not infringe patents owned by Masimo , rejecting the medical-monitoring company's bid for ​a renewed import ban on the tech giant's smartwatches.

item.title: IMF says prolonged increase in energy prices could boost inflation, lower growth
item.description: The IMF on Thursday said it was closely monitoring developments in the Iran war and the ​resulting disruptions to energy production, warning that prolonged increases in energy ‌prices could boost inflation and lower growth globally.

item.title: Cloud industry group calls for EU interim measure against Broadcom over VMware
item.description: Lobbying group Cloud Infrastructure Services Providers in Europe on Thursday urged EU ​antitrust regulators to temporarily stop Broadcom from ending ‌its VMware Cloud Service Provider programme in Europe, ramping up its fight against the U.S. tech company.

item.title: Explainer: How important is food to Unilever?
item.description: Unilever shares were down on Thursday, extending losses from Wednesday after a report the company may spin off its food business, and had held merger talks with Kraft Heinz that have ended.

item.title: US wholesale inventories fall sharply in January
item.description: U.S. wholesale inventories dropped sharply January, a trend that if sustained could ​result in inventory investment being a drag ‌on economic growth in the first quarter.

item.title: US new home sales drop to near 3-1/2-year low in January
item.description: Sales of new U.S. single-family homes fell more than expected in January, hitting their lowest level in nearly 3-1/2 years, likely weighed down by harsh winter weather.
